message:
feat(overprompting): upgrade to 3D visualizations and redesign OG images

Major enhancements to the overprompting article:

Visualizations (overprompting-visualizations.tsx):
- Upgrade constraint visualization from 2D SVG to 3D Three.js
- Add animated particle cloud that compresses as constraints increase
- Use React Three Fiber with Float, Stars, and custom particle system
- Add HUD overlay with constraint list and impact percentages
- Include level slider control and visual quality indicator
- Fix bufferAttribute args prop for R3F v8 compatibility

Article (overprompting-article.tsx):
- Enhanced layout and styling refinements
- Improved section transitions and animations

OG/Twitter Images:
- Redesigned from funnel to singularity visual
- Added concentric HUD rings for tech aesthetic
- Updated spacing and proportions
- Maintained Satori-safe patterns

Technical fixes:
- Use gl={{ alpha: true }} instead of deprecated alpha prop
- Add missing CONSTRAINT_LABELS and CONSTRAINT_IMPACT constants
- Fix bufferAttribute to use args=[array, itemSize] pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The chef wants to be helpful (assume you’re paying them a lot), but every time you add another one of your rules, you are restricting @@ -530,6 +615,59 @@ export function OverpromptingArticle() { + + + {/* ========== KEY TAKEAWAYS ========== */} +

+ +
+
+ +
+ +

Key Takeaways

+ +
+
+

The Problem

+
    +
  • + + Every constraint restricts the model’s creative search space. +
    • +
  • + + Detailed instructions often lead to “face-in-hole” outputs with no artistry. +
    • +
  • + + The more you tell a model how to do something, the less it focuses on the goal. +
    • +
+
+ +
+

The Solution

+
    +
  • + + Phase 1: Be open and intent-focused during planning. +
    • +
  • + + Phase 2: Be extremely precise once the plan is set. +
    • +
  • + + Trust the model’s latent space to find the best implementation path. +
    • +
+
+
+
+ +
+ {/* Footer spacing */}
